panels.description: Wide shot: Nighttime in an Indian jungle stockade. A massive wooden gate made of lashed logs crashes down, sealing in a herd of trumpeting wild elephants. In the foreground, the colossal elephant Kala Nag stands calm amidst the chaos, illuminated by the flickering orange glow of torches held by mahouts.

bubbles.text: After weeks of driving scattered elephants across the hills, the wild monsters were driven into the last stockade, the gate jarring down behind them.
Kala Nag would go into that flaring, trumpeting pandemonium to hustle the biggest tuskers into quiet.

panels.description: Dynamic medium shot, low angle: A flashback of Kala Nag's prowess. The great elephant uses a sickle-cut motion of his head to knock a leaping tiger sideways in mid-air. The tiger's claws are out, but Kala Nag's soft trunk is curled up and safe. Heavy shadows and action lines.

bubbles.text: There was nothing in the way of fighting that the old wise Black Snake did not know.

panels.description: Medium shot: Big Toomai sits confidently on the neck of Kala Nag. Below him, ten-year-old Little Toomai stands at his full height of four feet, looking up with a determined and proud expression. He wears only a simple rag and holds himself with natural authority.

bubbles.text: There is nothing that the Black Snake fears except me. He has seen three generations of us.
He is afraid of me also!

panels.description: Wide shot: The small boy, Little Toomai, takes bold strides toward the mountain-like elephant, Kala Nag. The elephant is obediently lifting one of its massive front feet as the boy commands him. The scale difference is immense, highlighting the boy's fearlessness.

bubbles.text: Yes, he is afraid of me! Lift up your feet, you fat old pig!

panels.description: Medium close-up: Little Toomai with a dreamy, excited look on his face, gesturing toward the horizon. In a faint, warm-toned visualization behind him, Kala Nag is shown adorned in a gold howdah and red silk, walking at the head of a royal procession.

bubbles.text: Thou wilt have nothing to do but carry gold earrings and a gold howdah at the head of the King's processions. Then I shall sit on thy neck with a silver ankus!

panels.description: Medium shot: Big Toomai looks tired and unimpressed, leaning back against a rough wooden post in the camp. He watches his son with a skeptical expression, the background showing the rugged reality of the elephant lines and jungle terrain.

bubbles.text: Umph! Thou art a boy, and as wild as a buffalo-calf. I am getting old. Give me brick elephant lines and broad roads instead of this camping.
